summary: The Middle East war has escalated with attacks on energy infrastructure and a possible prolonged closure of the Strait of Hormuz, triggering a sharp repricing in energy markets. Crude topping $110 is compounding inflation pressure as the Iran conflict drags on alongside U.S. tariffs raising costs.
tweet: The Iran war is pushing U.S. inflation well above Fed projections, with Fed funds futures now pricing a hike instead of a cut — fear is the dominant trade across every asset class.
